mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month contraction in industrial production reflects negative supply shocks that compress corporate earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations when INDPRO declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls reflects a joint contraction in income and spending that amplifies downside risk to corporate revenues and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In a predictive regression of 20-day S&P 500 returns on RSAFS, PAYEMS, and their interaction, the interaction coefficient is not significantly negative (two-tailed p-value > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, an elevated federal funds rate increases the equity discount rate and reduces the present value of future cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, the average S&P 500 5-day forward return is greater than or equal to zero, or a one-sided t-test of negative mean returns yields p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Declining new private housing units authorized reflects weakening residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials, household durables, and credit, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month change in HOUST coincides with positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when inflation exceeds a critical threshold.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 4.0%, the average S&P 500 5-day forward return is greater than or equal to zero over triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: In periods where the unemployment rate is rising and the federal funds rate remains elevated, the combination of tight credit and weakening labor income produces amplified negative wealth effects that drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In a regression of 20-day S&P 500 returns on UNRATE, FEDFUNDS, and their interaction, the interaction coefficient is not significantly negative (two-tailed p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP reflects an aggregate demand shortfall that reduces top-line revenue growth across the index and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 contraction is followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations.
